steps.title: Recognize what the rhetorical question implicitly answers
steps.content: The passage emphasizes the soul's unknowability—“the soul of a man is unknowable... the final mystery is oneself.” The underlined question, “Who can calculate the orbit of his own soul?” is rhetorical and implicitly answers no one can.

steps.title: Connect the implicit answer to the choices
steps.content:
Because the rhetorical question answers no one can, it reinforces the text's core skepticism about self-knowledge. The other choices either shift focus to readers' beliefs (B), assume the text provides instructional guidance (C), or mischaracterize the tone as reluctant concession (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
